entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: Learn typing skills with guided instruction. Includes games for practice and reinforcement. Students learn the position of each letter, number, and symbol on the keyboard and advance from typing individual letters to simple words, then paragraphs and documents. Fluency is encouraged through word-per-minute goals.

entries.title: Placement & Combining Tips
entries.content: The Typing.com website contains a placement test. Take this first, and then progress at your own pace.

Permission to print for non-commercial use. See Alveary group use policy to use lessons
in a group context.

LINKS: Click text or scan the QR code in the top corner of the lesson plan pages to view
online resources associated with the lessons.

Responsibility for previewing all links rests with the teacher. All links were checked at the
time of publication; however, websites change frequently and may contain objectionable
content. Please report broken links by contacting us through our website.

Keyboarding
☐ Make sure students have an ergonomically friendly place to type. Proper posture improves speed while avoiding repetitive movement injury. Ideally, elbows should be bent at a 90-degree angle and should rest on the table/desk with the screen at eye level. To attain proper alignment, adjust the chair's height or have students sit on a pillow, use a footstool, and/or place the computer on a stand.

☐ Be sure to view Extra Helpings for ideas on how to integrate Typing into your days and to explore resources for extra practice (such as a website where students practice typing classic literature).

blocks.text: Create (or have students create) a free account on typing.com, and have your student(s) take a few placement tests if they have any previous typing experience. You can find the site link in Quick Links.
Have students practice typing and ensure they use correct fingering.
As students gain fluency, they can start learning to type narrations. This may be especially helpful for students with learning differences.
Let students share any thoughts or connections they have about the lesson.

lessons.title: 30m Keyboarding - Lesson 1
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: Typing.com 

Keyboarding Lesson Tracker
⬚  ⬚  

PREP: Spend two days each week working for 30 min on keyboarding.

➜ INTRO
Watch any video tutorials that go with the current lesson.

➜ PRACTICE
Keeping fingers in their proper places on the home row, complete the exercise(s) for this lesson.

➜ PLAY
Use correct fingering to play any games that go with the lesson.
